description_long: This is a rustic wooden barn with a simple rectangular footprint and a steeply pitched gable roof covered in overlapping wooden shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontally laid logs, giving it a traditional, hand-built appearance. The barn sits on a low stone foundation, and the ground around it is grassy. One end features a single, centered wooden door, while the opposite end has a small, high window and visible wooden bracing. The structure is devoid of visible windows along the long sides, emphasizing its utilitarian, storage-focused design. The overall look is that of a historic or rural agricultural building, likely used for storing hay, tools, or livestock.
description: A rustic wooden barn with a steep gable roof, horizontal log walls, and a single front door. The barn sits on a stone foundation and features minimal windows, emphasizing its traditional, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long, horizontal log wall and the shingled gable roof, with a grassy base and no visible doors or windows.
description_view_1: End view featuring the main entrance: a single, centered wooden door set in a log wall, with the steep gable roof above.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the long log wall and roof, with a small stone foundation visible at the base.
description_view_3: Rear end view displaying a small, high window and wooden bracing under the gable, with no door present.
attribute_door_style: single hinged door, centered on the gable end
attribute_window_arrangement: one small, high window on the rear gable end; no windows on the long sides
